task_text:
Add wildcard negation support in test spec tag expressions

The test spec parser in `internal/catch_test_spec_parser.cpp` supports `[tag]` inclusion and `~[tag]` exclusion, but does not support negated wildcard patterns like `~[slow*]` to exclude all tags matching a prefix. The `TestSpec::TagPattern` in `catch_test_spec.hpp` performs an exact `std::find` for a tag literal and does not use `WildcardPattern`, so a pattern such as `[slow*]` or `~[slow*]` will only match a test whose tag is literally `"slow*"`. Extend `TagPattern::matches` in `catch_test_spec.cpp` to use `WildcardPattern` (from `internal/catch_wildcard_pattern.hpp`) when the tag string contains `*` characters, enabling wildcard inclusion and exclusion.
solve_notes: Added a wildcard path to `TagPattern::matches`: when `m_tag` contains `'*'`, construct a `WildcardPattern` with `CaseSensitive::No` and return true if `any_of` the test's tags matches. `WildcardPattern` and `CaseSensitive` are already available via `catch_test_spec.hpp` (which includes `catch_wildcard_pattern.hpp`). The existing `std::find` exact-match path is preserved as the fallback for tag strings without wildcards.

search_sequence.result: Uses `std::find` over `testCase.tags` with `Tag(m_tag)` — exact case-insensitive comparison; no wildcard support
search_sequence.reasoning: Task description pointed directly to this method

search_sequence.action: Read `NamePattern` in catch_test_spec.cpp (lines 31-43) as a wildcard model
search_sequence.result: Stores a `WildcardPattern m_wildcardPattern` and calls `m_wildcardPattern.matches(testCase.name)` — demonstrates the API
search_sequence.reasoning: NamePattern already supports wildcards; this provides the exact pattern to follow for TagPattern

search_sequence.result: `WildcardPattern(std::string pattern, CaseSensitive)` + `bool matches(std::string const&)` — simple constructor+method pair
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to confirm the constructor signature and whether case sensitivity is a ctor or method argument

search_sequence.result: `#include <catch2/internal/catch_wildcard_pattern.hpp>` already present at line 17; `WildcardPattern` and `CaseSensitive` available without new includes
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to verify no new includes required in the .cpp

search_sequence.result: `Tag::original` is `StringRef`; must be explicitly converted to `std::string` before passing to `WildcardPattern::matches`
search_sequence.reasoning: WildcardPattern::matches takes `std::string const&`; StringRef is not implicitly convertible

key_decisions.decision: Add a wildcard branch that constructs WildcardPattern on the fly rather than storing it as a field
alternatives: Store WildcardPattern in TagPattern as NamePattern does for every pattern
Pre-detect wildcard in constructor and store a flag
key_decisions.reasoning: Most tag patterns are non-wildcard; constructing WildcardPattern only when needed keeps the common path as a simple std::find call with no extra allocation

exploration_log.aha_moment: Seeing that `catch_test_spec.hpp` already includes `catch_wildcard_pattern.hpp` meant the entire fix was a single modification to the .cpp — no header changes needed.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have gone straight to catch_test_spec.hpp to check its includes before reading NamePattern's implementation; that would have confirmed the API availability faster.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: TestSpec::TagPattern::matches
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: added a wildcard branch using WildcardPattern before the existing std::find exact-match path

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: WildcardPattern
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: read: needed to confirm constructor signature WildcardPattern(string, CaseSensitive) and matches(string const&) method before writing the wildcard branch

thrash_preventing_defs.name: TestSpec::NamePattern::matches
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: provides the exact code pattern to follow — m_wildcardPattern.matches(testCase.name) — preventing the agent from inventing a different API usage or searching for examples

thrash_preventing_defs.name: Tag
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to know that Tag::original is StringRef (not std::string) so the correct conversion to std::string is required when calling WildcardPattern::matches

thrash_preventing_defs.name: TagPattern
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirmed m_tag is std::string; also confirmed WildcardPattern is available via the header's existing includes

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient is the edited method plus the WildcardPattern class (required to know the API). thrash_preventing adds NamePattern::matches (the code template to follow), Tag struct (so the agent knows StringRef conversion is needed), and TagPattern class declaration (to confirm m_tag type and available includes). Without these an agent would guess at API shapes and miss the StringRef-to-string conversion.

excluded_defs.name: TestSpecParser::visitPatternExpr
excluded_defs.reason: task mentions catch_test_spec_parser.cpp but the actual change is entirely in catch_test_spec.cpp; the parser correctly constructs TagPattern and does not need to change

queries.query_text: Test filter tags support exact string matching but do not support pattern matching with wildcards so it is impossible to exclude a group of related tags with a single filter expression
queries.justification: Rule compliance: uses domain terms 'test filter tags', 'exact string matching', 'pattern matching', 'wildcards', 'group of related tags' — no symbol names. Pre-implementation: a developer wanting wildcard tag exclusion would describe the limitation this way.

Rule compliance: 'Tag( m_tag )' is the literal expression in the original source at the exact line being changed. Pre-implementation: a developer searching for the tag comparison logic would search for this expression.
Rule compliance: five exact symbol names from the diff. Pre-implementation: a developer tracing the tag matching logic would identify these symbols first.

Rule compliance: two explicit file paths from the solution. Pre-implementation: a developer adding wildcard support to tag matching would navigate to the test spec implementation and the wildcard pattern class.

queries.justification: Rule compliance: mixes domain concept 'wildcard tag filtering' with exact symbols 'TagPattern::matches' and 'WildcardPattern'. Pre-implementation: a developer who has identified the pattern type and wildcard class would frame the task this way.

queries.query_text: Add wildcard support to TagPattern::matches in catch_test_spec.cpp: when m_tag contains '*' use WildcardPattern to match over testCase.tags instead of exact std::find
queries.justification: Rule compliance: includes symbols, file path, and domain context about the fix. Pre-implementation: this is the natural full query after identifying the method and the required change.

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of TestSpec::TagPattern::matches in src/catch2/catch_test_spec.cpp

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Contains multiple SECTION blocks that call spec.matches() after parsing tag expressions like '[one]', '[x]', '~[one]', which drives TagPattern::matches through the changed lines
